description: Fed maintains accommodative policy during COVID-19; gold price begins upward trajectory.
RBZ rolls out strict access point geolocation directives; macro liquidity constraints prioritize formal gold extraction as a vital central export earner.
COVID-19 pandemic declared; global lockdowns restrict chemical imports and ground regional safe-haven bullion flights.
S.I. 80 of 2020 gazetted: Banking regulations enforce tight mobile money limits and interoperability rules to disrupt parallel market cash pools.
FGR Restructuring: Implements a 100% USD cash payment guarantee for artisanal miners to capture spot flows.
Primary Producer Split Rule: Mandates a restrictive 55% USD and 45% local currency (ZWL) split for corporate mines.
Regional tensions begin affecting shipping insurance and logistics costs.
Launch of the FX Auction System: Formalizes retention at 70% USD / 30% ZWL, but forces conversion at heavily discounted official rates.
Harmonized transit quotas and pandemic screening metrics enforce physical tracking delays at Beitbridge border.
Fed maintains low interest rates; gold remains highly attractive.
Sustained domestic foreign currency shortages; small-scale tracking operations ramp up amid parallel market forex premiums.
Incremental Export Incentive Scheme (IEIS) introduced via Circular No. 4; baseline corporate retention remains 60%, but incremental volumes yield 80% USD retention and offshore borrowing rights.
Central bank gold purchases remain elevated post-pandemic.
Baseline status quo period; monetary regime holds currency retention splits steady across mining tiers.
Russia-Ukraine war triggers massive safe-haven buying and G7 sanctions, causing global energy, fuel, and chemical prices to surge.
US Federal Reserve starts aggressive interest rate hiking cycle; strengthens USD and penalizes developing market assets.
Central banks accelerate gold reserve accumulation amid geopolitical tensions.
UAE launches mandatory Good Delivery Standard for Gold; enforces strict audit tracking and beneficial ownership checks on bullion entering Dubai refiners.
Fed rate hike cycle continues; gold price volatility increases.
Exchange Control Directive RY002/2023: Abolishes the 100% USD ASM tier and discontinues the IEIS corporate bonuses. Standardizes a flat 75% USD and 25% local currency retention across all sectors.
Middle East shipping disruptions begin affecting African export routes.
Record central bank purchases; China accelerates gold reserve accumulation aggressively.
Middle East geopolitical escalation triggers safe-haven buying, driving spot gold values toward $2000/oz.
Enforcement of MPC Directive: Systematically strips out special economic zone dispensations or debt-servicing conversion exemptions to retain domestic liquid forex reserves.
Fed signals rate cut pivot; gold price begins steep upward ascent.
2024 National Budget takes effect: Broadens the tax base and eliminates key corporate VAT exemptions to push formal sector compliance.
Aggressive monetary tightening cycle: Central bank holds high policy interest rates and statutory reserve requirements to defend the currency.
US terminates Executive Order sanctions on Zimbabwe, shifting to targeted Global Magnitsky listings. Simultaneously, Gulf of Aden disruptions worsen; vessels reroute around Cape of Good Hope.
2024 Monetary Policy Statement: Discontinues the collapsing ZWL and introduces the gold-backed ZiG. Retention remains 75% USD, but the 25% local leg is paid in volatile ZiG.
FATF expands compliance audits over regional cash transfer nodes, restricting anonymous regional dollar pooling.
Central bank purchases reach nearly 1,000 tonnes across H2 2024-H1 2025.
Fed cuts rates three times in 2024; gold price surges strongly.
Launch of the digital 'Mine-to-Point-of-Sale' tracking framework, tightening mineral logistics mapping from delivery sites to the refinery.
Trade uncertainty and tariff announcements increase global shipping costs and supply friction.
Ongoing war takes toll on Zimbabwe-UAE gold trade; shipping costs rise 5x. Simultaneously, SARS deploys advanced border scanners at Beitbridge to target illicit metal transit.
Global gold demand hits record 5,002 tonnes in 2025.
Strategic raw mineral export ban escalations; deepens state enforcement push for local beneficiation and value addition.
2025 Monetary Policy Statement: Compresses export retention down to 70% USD and 30% ZiG across all tiers to feed the interbank market, heightening parallel diversion risks.
S.I. 17 of 2025 gazetted: Amends the 2020 framework, sharply shifting licensing/turnover fee structures for mobile payment engines.
Gold price surpasses $5,000/oz; reflects a 22% rise in 2025 following a 64% increase in 2024.
ETF inflows reach 801 tonnes; investment demand surges 84% YoY. LBMA introduces Responsible Gold Guidance V10 adding strict tracking parameters.
Mines and Minerals Amendment Bill officially gazetted: Mandates a digital mining cadastre system, designates strategic minerals, and restructures land tenure boundaries.
Fed cuts rates again in September 2025; gold continues upward trajectory.
Geopolitical tensions remain high; gold price hits 53 new all-time highs.
Zimbabwe raises gold royalty to 10% when prices exceed $2,501/oz; miners warn of increased smuggling risks.
Zimbabwe enforces raw mineral export restrictions including raw lithium blocks; affects overall mining sector logistics.
